target.title: fix compaction timeout partial progress
target.body:
## Summary

- Preserve completed chunk summaries when a later compaction chunk times out, so timeout recovery can reuse partial progress instead of falling back to a generic “summary unavailable” note and restarting from chunk 1 on the next attempt.
- Preserve cooperative partial progress through the outer `compactWithSafetyTimeout()` wall-clock timeout path by allowing the canceled native compaction to settle with its cancellation-produced result before the timeout error wins the race.
- Wire the safety-timeout reason through the real native `activeSession.compact()` / `activeSession.abortCompaction(reason)` path, so `AgentSession` can commit a result that was produced by timeout cancellation while still treating non-timeout aborts as cancellation.
- Keep the timeout-result acceptance window alive through the native `AgentSession` post-await commit continuation, then expire it once the safety wrapper rejects so a late-settling timeout-aborted compaction cannot append session state after the run is already failed.
- Remove the old `src/node-host/with-timeout.ts` helper after this compaction path stopped using it, which fixes the `check-dependencies` deadcode CI failure for the updated branch.
- Keep caller abort cancellation semantics intact: non-timeout aborts still do not get converted into successful partial summaries.
- Surface compaction timeout details in the preflight failure reply, including the existing `agents.defaults.compaction.timeoutSeconds` knob, while generic non-verbose compaction failures still hide details.

Fixes #92043

## Maintainer-ready notes

- **Why it matters / User impact:** Users with large transcripts can hit the default compaction wall-clock timeout after some chunks already summarized successfully. Before this patch, that useful progress was discarded and the next attempt repeated the same earlier chunks, creating a permanent failure loop. The preflight reply also hid that the failure was a timeout, leaving users without the relevant config knob.
- **Fix classification:** Root-cause reliability fix for compaction timeout progress reuse and timeout failure copy.
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** High for the local source/runtime surface covered here. The change has RED/GREEN regression coverage for inner chunk timeout, outer safety-timeout cancellation, and user-facing timeout copy; it preserves abort cancellation behavior and does not alter default timeout values or public config shape.
- **Root cause:** The root source was split across three timeout boundaries. `summarizeChunks()` discarded completed chunk summaries on timeout-shaped failures; the outer `compactWithSafetyTimeout()` could reject with its wall-clock timeout before a cooperating native compaction returned the partial result it produced during timeout cancellation; the real native `AgentSession` path aborted compaction without preserving the timeout reason, so a result produced after timeout cancellation was still classified as plain aborted work; the timeout-abort classification originally accepted any matching error name, which left a late-settling timeout result able to append after the safety wrapper had already rejected; and the first bounded-acceptance implementation expired the token before `AgentSession`'s post-await commit gate could observe it.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** This changes the owners that enforce the partial-progress invariant: `src/agents/compaction.ts` carries partial summaries from inner chunk failures, `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/compaction-safety-timeout.ts` preserves the timeout reason, gives the native post-await commit continuation a bounded chance to return cancellation-produced progress before the timeout rejection wins, and expires timeout-result acceptance once the wrapper rejects; `src/agents/sessions/agent-session.ts` commits a compact result only while that timeout reason is still accepted instead of accepting arbitrary late timeout-named aborts. The copy change stays at the preflight compaction failure formatter.
- **What did NOT change:** Chunk planning, model retry policy, compaction timeout defaults, persistent session storage, provider routing, public config shape, and unrelated compaction behavior are unchanged and out of scope.
- **Architecture / source-of-truth check:** `src/agents/compaction.ts` remains the source-of-truth owner for summary partial-progress behavior, `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/compaction-safety-timeout.ts` owns the native compaction wall-clock race, and `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.ts` remains the source-of-truth owner for preflight agent-run failure copy.
- **Patch quality notes:** Timeout recovery is bounded to cooperative cancellation settlement at the timeout boundary and to the active safety-wrapper acceptance window through the native commit continuation. Non-timeout aborts still reject promptly, non-settling compactions still reject with `Compaction timed out`, late timeout-aborted results after wrapper rejection are ignored by `AgentSession`, and the default 180s timeout is unchanged.
- **Target test file:** `src/agents/compaction-partial-summary.test.ts`, `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner.compaction-safety-timeout.test.ts`, `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/compact.hooks.test.ts`, and `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-direct-runtime-config.test.ts`.
- **Scenario locked in:** Regression test scenarios: a two-chunk compaction where chunk 1 succeeds and chunk 2 throws `TimeoutError` must return the chunk 1 summary plus a `[Partial summary: ...]` marker; a compaction canceled by outer timeout and immediately resolving with progress must return that progress instead of `Compaction timed out`; direct native compaction passes the safety timeout reason into `activeSession.abortCompaction(reason)` so timeout-aborted results can be committed only while the safety wrapper still accepts that reason; a late result after wrapper rejection must not remain accepted; a preflight compaction timeout must surface the timeout reason and config guidance.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** The regression tests exercise the source owner boundaries directly and keep existing abort/timeout tests in place so timeout recovery cannot convert user cancellation or hung compaction into success.
- **Out of scope:** This PR does not change the default 180s timeout, add persistent checkpoint storage, change the shape/default of `agents.defaults.compaction.timeoutSeconds`, alter compaction chunk planning, or change model/provider retry policy.

## Root Cause

`src/agents/compaction.ts` treated timeout errors the same as cancellation and rethrew immediately before attaching the already-generated summary to the error. `summarizeWithFallback()` therefore had no partial summary to reuse, even when earlier chunks had already completed successfully.

The outer safety timeout added a second loss path: `compactWithSafetyTimeout()` raced native compaction against a timeout rejection. On timeout it aborted the active compaction, but the timeout rejection could win before the native compaction settled with the partial result it produced during that cancellation.

The native caller added a third loss path: `compactEmbeddedAgentSessionDirectOnce()` canceled the real `AgentSession` via `activeSession.abortCompaction()` with no timeout reason, and `AgentSession.runCompactionWork()` discarded any compact result once its internal compaction signal was aborted. Timeout cancellation and user/request abort therefore collapsed into the same aborted state.

Separately, `buildPreflightCompactionFailureText()` hid the preflight compaction reason unless verbose details were enabled. That made a 180s compaction timeout look like a generic unrecoverable compaction failure and did not point users at the configured timeout knob.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Later chunk timeout and outer wall-clock safety timeout no longer discard already-generated compaction summary progress; preflight compaction timeout replies now show the timeout reason and `agents.defaults.compaction.timeoutSeconds` guidance.
- **Real environment tested:** Local Linux OpenClaw worktree at `/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/issue-92043`, branch `feat/issue-92043`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
cd /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/issue-92043 && OPENCLAW_REPO=/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/issue-92043 node --import tsx /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-issue-92043-evidence/compaction-real-wallclock-proof.mjs > /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-issue-92043-evidence/compaction-real-wallclock-proof.out.json
```

```bash
cd /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/issue-92043 && OPENCLAW_TEST_FAST=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=240000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner.compaction-safety-timeout.test.ts -t "keeps timeout acceptance alive through the native commit continuation"
```

```bash
cd /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/issue-92043 && OPENCLAW_TEST_FAST=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=240000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner.compaction-safety-timeout.test.ts src/agents/compaction-partial-summary.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/compact.hooks.test.ts
```

```bash
cd /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/issue-92043 && node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --noEmit
```

```bash
cd /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/issue-92043 && ./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/compaction-safety-timeout.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner.compaction-safety-timeout.test.ts
```

```bash
git -C /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/issue-92043 diff --check
```

- **Evidence after fix:**

```json
{
  "proof": "compaction-real-wallclock-timeout",
  "head": "5013dda6bd70253d7614c12257a8e7a3b0e94c03",
  "timerMode": "real setTimeout; no fake timers; no Vitest",
  "timeoutMs": 120,
  "elapsedMs": 123,
  "result": "partial summary after chunk 1 survived real wall-clock safety timeout",
  "events": [
    { "event": "compaction-start", "aborted": false },
    {
      "event": "timeout-abort-observed",
      "reasonName": "CompactionTimeoutError",
      "reasonMessage": "Compaction timed out"
    },
    {
      "event": "onCancel",
      "reasonName": "CompactionTimeoutError",
      "reasonMessage": "Compaction timed out"
    },
    { "event": "partial-summary-committed-after-timeout-abort" }
  ]
}
```

```text
Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests  2 passed | 48 skipped (50)
```

```text
Test Files  3 passed (3)
Tests  103 passed (103)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 20.81s
```

```text
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --noEmit exited 0 with no stdout/stderr output.
```

```text
All matched files use the correct format.
Finished in 21ms on 2 files using 1 threads.
```

```text
git diff --check exited 0 with no whitespace errors.
```

- **Observed result after fix:** A later timeout after chunk 1 completes returns the chunk 1 summary with a `[Partial summary: chunks 1-1 of 2 were summarized...]` marker instead of the generic unavailable summary. The real wall-clock proof ran the production `compactWithSafetyTimeout()` wrapper outside Vitest/fake timers, observed a real timeout abort with `CompactionTimeoutError`, called `onCancel` with that reason, and returned `partial summary after chunk 1 survived real wall-clock safety timeout`. The direct native caller regression verifies the safety timeout reason is passed into `activeSession.abortCompaction(reason)`, and the new native commit-continuation regression verifies that a result produced after timeout cancellation still sees its timeout reason accepted at the `AgentSession`-shaped post-await gate before the safety wrapper rejects. The focused late-result regression remains covered by the full compaction suite, verifying acceptance expires after wrapper rejection so a late timeout-aborted result cannot append session state after the timed-out run has already failed. Hung compactions still reject with `Compaction timed out`; run-level aborts still reject as aborts.
- **What was not tested:** No live external model provider, full 180s gateway compaction, Desktop session, or external channel transport was exercised. The added proof uses the production safety-timeout wrapper with real wall-clock timers and no Vitest/fake timers; the native `activeSession.abortCompaction(reason)` handoff remains covered by focused direct-runtime regression tests.

## Regression Test Plan

- `src/agents/compaction-partial-summary.test.ts` locks the inner timeout case: once a chunk has completed, a later timeout preserves the latest summary and partial marker.
- `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner.compaction-safety-timeout.test.ts` locks the outer safety-timeout race: timeout cancellation can return cooperative partial progress before the timeout error wins, native post-await commit continuation still sees an accepted timeout reason, timeout/external abort reasons are threaded into the cancel hook, and timeout-result acceptance expires once the wrapper rejects.
- `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/compact.hooks.test.ts` locks the real direct native caller: safety timeout cancellation passes the timeout reason into `activeSession.abortCompaction(reason)` before the native compact result is returned.
- The existing abort and hung-timeout tests remain green: non-timeout aborts still do not get converted into partial-success summaries, and non-settling compactions still reject with timeout.

## Merge risk

- **Risk labels considered:** session-state, reliability, user-facing copy, timeout handling.
- **Risk explanation:** The behavior change is limited to compaction timeout errors after useful progress exists or when a cooperating compaction settles because of timeout cancellation before the safety wrapper rejects. First-chunk failures still fall through to the existing generic fallback, hung compactions still time out, late timeout-aborted results after wrapper rejection are ignored, and non-timeout aborts still propagate as cancellation.
- **Why acceptable:** Reusing completed chunk summaries makes repeated compaction attempts move forward instead of discarding progress. The copy change only reveals timeout reasons for compaction timeout failures; generic non-verbose compaction errors remain redacted.
- **Out-of-scope boundary:** This PR does not change the default 180s timeout, add persistent checkpoint storage, alter compaction chunk planning, or change model/provider retry policy.

target.title: fix(doctor): suppress false group-allowlist warning when all accounts override groupAllowFrom (fixes #92684)
target.body:
### Summary

- **Problem**: `openclaw doctor` over-warns about an empty top-level `groupAllowFrom` when every sub-account defines its own populated `groupAllowFrom`. The warning claims group messages will be silently dropped, but at runtime `account.groupAllowFrom ?? parent.groupAllowFrom` means the empty top-level fallback is never consulted.
- **Root Cause**: `scanEmptyAllowlistPolicyWarnings` in `empty-allowlist-scan.ts` unconditionally scans the top-level channel config as if it were an account, without checking whether sub-accounts already override `groupAllowFrom`. Both the shared default group-allowlist warning path and the channel plugin `extraWarningsForAccount` path were un-gated.
- **Fix**: Before scanning the top-level record, check whether every active sub-account already supplies its own `groupAllowFrom`. If all accounts override it, suppress both the default group-allowlist warning hook and the `extraWarningsForAccount` plugin hook for the top-level call (the skip is applied only when `parent` is undefined, so account-level checks are unaffected). The existing per-account scan with parent-fallback resolution still catches accounts that genuinely lack a populated allowlist.
- **What changed**:
  - `src/commands/doctor/shared/empty-allowlist-scan.ts` — added pre-scan of sub-account `groupAllowFrom`, wrapped both `shouldSkipDefaultEmptyGroupAllowlistWarning` and `extraWarningsForAccount` hooks when all accounts override
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary)**:
  - DM-policy (`dmPolicy: "allowlist"`) handling — the top-level DM warning is still emitted; only the group-allowlist false positive is addressed
  - Channel plugin custom skip hooks for account-level calls — the wrapped hooks delegate to the original for non-top-level calls
  - Any runtime message-delivery logic — this is a doctor advisory fix only
  - `allowFrom`-as-group-coverage accounting — the channel's `groupAllowFromFallbackToAllowFrom` heuristic is left to future work; the current fix is conservative and only suppresses when every account has an explicit `groupAllowFrom` list

### Reproduction

1. Configure a channel with `groupPolicy: "allowlist"`, empty top-level `groupAllowFrom`, and two or more accounts each with a populated `groupAllowFrom`.
2. Run `openclaw doctor`.
3. **Before this PR**: doctor emits a top-level group-allowlist warning claiming group messages will be dropped, even though every account overrides the fallback and inbound group messages are processed normally.
4. **After this PR**: the top-level group-allowlist warning is suppressed when all accounts supply their own `groupAllowFrom`; warnings still fire when no accounts exist or when some accounts lack `groupAllowFrom` (requiring fallback to the empty top-level).

### Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed (92684)**: doctor `EmptyAllowlistCheck` warns the top-level record as if it were an account when `groupPolicy: "allowlist"` and `groupAllowFrom` is empty, even though every sub-account defines its own `groupAllowFrom`. The fix suppresses that false positive on both the shared default warning path and the channel plugin extra-warning path, while preserving the warning when accounts genuinely lack a populated allowlist.

**Real environment tested**: Linux, Node 22 — direct scanner invocation loaded through the real `createChannelDoctorEmptyAllowlistPolicyHooks` framework (no mocks, not a Vitest test)

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**: `node --import tsx scripts/direct-scanner-proof-92684.mjs`

**Evidence after fix**:
```text
=== Real scanner proof: #92684 (direct, non-test invocation) ===
Config: telegram groupPolicy=allowlist, top-level groupAllowFrom=[]
        work.groupAllowFrom=["@friend1"], personal.groupAllowFrom=["@friend2"]

Total warnings: 2
Top-level group false positives: 0
Result: ✅ No false positive — all accounts override groupAllowFrom

Regression (no accounts): top-level group warnings = 1 ✅ still warns
```

**Observed result after fix**: direct scanner invocation with the real doctor hooks framework confirms zero top-level group-allowlist false positives when both accounts supply their own `groupAllowFrom`. The two remaining warnings are account-level Telegram first-time-setup advisories (not false positives), and the regression check confirms the top-level warning still fires when no accounts exist to override it.

**What was not tested**: live `openclaw doctor` CLI invocation against a fully provisioned OpenClaw installation was not driven — the scanner is exercised directly through the real channel-doctor hooks framework rather than through the full CLI pipeline.

**Repro confirmation**: the direct scanner script fails on origin/main (emits 1 top-level group-allowlist false positive through the Telegram extra-warning path) and succeeds after the third patch commit (emits 0 top-level group false positives, gating both the shared and extra-warning paths).

### Risk / Mitigation

- **Risk**: the wrapped `shouldSkipDefaultEmptyGroupAllowlistWarning` and `extraWarningsForAccount` hooks are restored immediately after the top-level `checkAccount` call, but the function mutates `params` directly.
  **Mitigation**: original function references are saved before the wrap and unconditionally restored on the next line, so account-level checks always receive the original hooks.
- **Risk**: a channel with a mix of accounts where some have `groupAllowFrom` and others do not could lose the top-level warning.
  **Mitigation**: the suppress condition requires **every** active sub-account to have a populated `groupAllowFrom` — a single account without it keeps both hook paths intact.
- **Risk**: a channel that supports `groupAllowFromFallbackToAllowFrom` might have accounts whose `allowFrom` provides effective group coverage without an explicit `groupAllowFrom`.
  **Mitigation**: this is deferred to future work; the current fix is conservative — it only suppresses when every account has an explicit `groupAllowFrom` list, so no valid warning is lost.

### Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix

### Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] CLI / doctor commands
- [x] Commands (doctor shared)

### Regression Test Plan

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/shared/empty-allowlist-scan.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/shared/empty-allowlist-policy.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/shared/empty-allowlist-scan-92684.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/shared/preview-warnings.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/repair-sequencing.test.ts`

### Review Findings Addressed

- **[P2] Gate the Telegram extra-warning path** — fixed in 7538369: `extraWarningsForAccount` is now wrapped alongside `shouldSkipDefaultEmptyGroupAllowlistWarning`, suppressing the top-level call when all accounts override. Confirmed by direct scanner proof (0 false positives vs 1 on main).
- **[P2] Count account allowFrom fallback coverage** — deferred; the current fix is conservative (requires explicit `groupAllowFrom` on every account). Extending to `allowFrom`-as-group-coverage requires channel-specific `groupAllowFromFallbackToAllowFrom` awareness and is left to future work.

### Linked Issue/PR

Fixes #92684

target.title: fix(telegram): respect group-level groupPolicy: open for /models callbacks in forum topics
target.body:
## Problem

In Telegram forum topics, clicking provider/model buttons from the `/models` inline menu silently does nothing when:
- the account-level `channels.telegram.groupPolicy` is `"allowlist"`,
- `channels.telegram.groupAllowFrom` is empty, and
- the specific group is configured with `groupPolicy: "open"` under `channels.telegram.groups.<chatId>`.

Native commands work in that same chat, but the model callback path drops the update without any visible error.

## Root cause

`isTelegramModelCallbackAuthorized` in `extensions/telegram/src/bot-handlers.runtime.ts` only evaluated the account-level allowlist via `resolveTelegramCommandIngressAuthorization`. It never consulted the per-group `groupPolicy` override, so a group explicitly marked `"open"` was still rejected because the global `groupAllowFrom` was empty.

## Fix

Add a group policy evaluation step before the existing fallback, mirroring what native commands already do:
- Pass `useTopicAndGroupOverrides: true` so `groupConfig.groupPolicy` is honored.
- Pass `allowEmptyAllowlistEntries: true` and `checkChatAllowlist: true` so a group listed in `groups` is treated as explicitly allowed even when the global allowlist is empty.
- Short-circuit with `true` when the effective policy is `"open"`.
- If the policy evaluation rejects, log the reason and return `false`.

## Regression test

Added `extensions/telegram/src/bot.create-telegram-bot.forum-models.test.ts` (collected `*.test.ts` filename) with two forum-topic cases:
- `mdl_list_openai_1` verifies the original message is edited into the provider's model list.
- `mdl_sel_openai/gpt-5.4` verifies the model selection callback updates the message with the success text.

## Verification

Full telegram extension suite:

```
npx vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-telegram.config.ts extensions/telegram/src/

Test Files  120 passed (120)
     Tests  2072 passed (2072)
Duration  87.94s
```

Focused regression test:

```
npx vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-telegram.config.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot.create-telegram-bot.forum-models.test.ts

Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  2 passed (2)
```

## Real Telegram proof

See PR comment below for terminal-based proof that `/models` provider/model buttons update the message after the patch.

Fixes #92655

target.title: fix(usage): make built-in footer easier to wrap on Telegram
target.body:
## Summary
- Simplify the built-in `/usage full` default footer so Telegram has a cleaner wrap point.
- Keep the model/provider/status badges compact and glue the context meter label to the meter.
- Remove the default token-count and cache-percentage atoms from the stock footer; custom templates can still add them back.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: the built-in `/usage full` default footer should be easier to read on Telegram/mobile surfaces, with the footer on its own line and a cleaner wrap point before the context meter instead of arbitrary breaks around the meter.

Real environment tested: MarvinMBP live OpenClaw gateway, real Telegram direct chat, running the patched dist built from the matching source change before this source-only PR was extracted.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Patched the live default template, hard-restarted the gateway with `launchctl kickstart -k`, sent real Telegram messages through the gateway, and asked Peter to inspect the resulting mobile Telegram footer wrap. Then extracted the same `src/auto-reply/usage-bar/default-template.ts` change onto this clean PR branch.

Evidence after fix: Copied live Telegram-observed footer shape from the verified turn:

```text
pioneer🤖opus48📌🌗⚡️ |
📚[⣿⣿⡀⠐⠐]1000k 💰0.0035
```

Peter's live verdict after checking the patched Telegram output: "That's acceptable. Please make a PR with that minor adjustment. Then I can forget about it and put my own custom footer back :)"

Observed result after fix: The footer rendered as an acceptable mobile Telegram default. The books/meter section still can wrap on very narrow displays, but the v2 default is materially cleaner and acceptable for the stock template.

What was not tested: No isolated Telegram bot-to-bot proof run for this PR head. The behavior was proven in the live local gateway before extraction. Discord was not re-proven in this PR turn beyond preserving the same `-#` surface shape with the simplified atoms. Custom `messages.usageTemplate` overrides are unaffected by this source-only default-template change.

## Verification
- Focused usage-bar tests passed locally: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts -- src/auto-reply/usage-bar`
- Reply-agent footer tests passed locally: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.misc.runreplyagent.test.ts`
- `pnpm oxfmt src/auto-reply/usage-bar/default-template.ts` reported the file clean.
- `pnpm tsgo` was attempted locally; it hit pre-existing unrelated main-branch TypeScript errors outside this file. The changed default-template file and focused usage-bar tests are clean.

## Notes
This is intentionally a conservative default-footer adjustment, not a template-engine change. It trades some dense telemetry (`↕️` token counts and `🗄` cache percentage) for better mobile readability in Telegram. Users who want the denser version can keep using a custom `messages.usageTemplate`.

target.title: fix(qqbot): allow scoped sandbox media sends
target.body:
## Summary

- Fix classification: Root-cause fix.
- Maintainer-ready confidence: High for first-pass review because the current head has fresh QQBot/Core regression tests, including host-read voice/audio coverage, plus build, `check:test-types`, and real QQBot C2C text/media runtime proof.
- Problem: sandbox-enabled agents can produce media paths such as `/workspace/...`, but QQBot outbound sends dropped the scoped media access passed by core and rejected those files before reaching the QQBot API.
- Why it matters / User impact: users could ask an agent to create a file in its workspace and send it through QQBot, yet the send failed before upload even though core had already granted a scoped media root for that workspace file.
- Solution: thread core-provided scoped media roots and `mediaAccess.readFile` through every QQBot outbound path while keeping existing root containment checks.
- Why this is root-cause fix: the source invariant is that core owns the sandbox media-access contract and each channel must preserve that contract to the low-level media resolver; the patch fixes the QQBot propagation break at every outbound entry point instead of adding a new fallback path or bypassing containment.
- What changed: QQBot outbound text/media paths (direct send, gateway dispatch, tool media forwarding, official C2C streaming, `<qqmedia>` tags, structured `QQBOT_PAYLOAD`, missing-voice staging, and host-read voice/audio staging) now preserve `mediaAccess`/`mediaLocalRoots`/`mediaReadFile`, and low-level senders consume core `mediaAccess.readFile` through the shared outbound loader with the existing explicit-`localRoots` guard. Relative media paths are now resolved against `mediaAccess.workspaceDir`. Host-read voice/audio is first read through the scoped loader into QQBot-managed media storage, then reuses the existing SILK transcode/upload path so QQ-required format conversion is not bypassed.
- What did NOT change: scope boundary unchanged; only QQBot's consumption of existing scoped media access changed. No broad host-file access was added, no QQBot credential/config shape changed, no public plugin SDK boundary was widened, and WebChat production code was not changed.
- Architecture / source-of-truth check: core outbound media context remains the source-of-truth contract for sandbox workspace access; QQBot now preserves that contract instead of inventing a channel-specific host-file permission path, and the plugin SDK security-runtime/localRoots validation remains the boundary for host reads.
- Related open PR / same-contract scan: this is the existing PR #92872 for linked issue #92827; the conflict-resolution update does not claim or introduce a broader public contract, schema, config, protocol, migration, or same-contract canonical PR shape.
- Patch quality notes: the catch/default-return warnings are limited to preserving existing `OutboundResult.error` behavior for host-read and QQ upload failures; they are not a silent fallback because upload-quota metadata still returns through the quota-specific result path and regression tests cover the error conversion.
- Risk labels considered: merge risk categories are message-delivery, security-boundary, compatibility, session-state, availability, and automation.
- Risk explanation: the highest merge risk is scoped QQBot host-media delivery, because the patch touches message dispatch and a sandbox/security boundary while rebasing onto latest `main` QQBot markdown chunking.
- Why acceptable: the risk is acceptable for maintainer review because every host read still requires explicit scoped `localRoots`/`mediaAccess`, no credential/config/public SDK behavior is widened, group-chat and other channels stay out of scope, and the current head passes focused QQBot/Core regressions plus build/type checks.

Fixes #92827

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: sandbox-created files referenced as `/workspace/...` can be sent/staged through WebChat and QQBot paths instead of being blocked by local media-root checks.

- Real environment tested: Linux local OpenClaw gateway, PR head `0bd19b9`, isolated runtime config, QQBot enabled with live credentials supplied through an environment SecretRef. Current conflict-resolution head `c850985` was revalidated against latest `main` with the regression/build/type checks below.

- Exact steps or command run after this patch: exact commands run for this PR and the current conflict-resolution head `c850985`:
```bash
node openclaw.mjs gateway run --port 19772 --bind loopback --auth none --verbose --force
node openclaw.mjs message send --channel qqbot --target "qqbot:c2c:<redacted>" --message "OpenClaw PR #92872 real-recipient QQ text proof sha=0bd19b9"
node openclaw.mjs message send --channel qqbot --target "qqbot:c2c:<redacted>" --message "OpenClaw PR #92872 real-recipient QQ media proof sha=0bd19b9" --media "pr-92872-qqbot-proof.txt"
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/qqbot/src/channel.message-adapter.test.ts extensions/qqbot/src/engine/gateway/outbound-dispatch.test.ts extensions/qqbot/src/engine/messaging/outbound-media-send.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/outbound/message-action-params.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.media.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat-reply-media.test.ts
corepack pnpm build
corepack pnpm check:test-types
```

- Evidence after fix:
```text
Gateway / QQBot startup:
[gateway] http server listening (9 plugins: acpx, browser, canvas, device-pair, file-transfer, memory-core, phone-control, qqbot, talk-voice)
[qqbot] [default] [qqbot:token:<redacted-appid>] <<< Body: {"access_token":"***","expires_in":"1806"}
[qqbot] [default] Access token obtained successfully
[qqbot] [default] WebSocket connected
[qqbot] [qqbot:default] Gateway ready

Real C2C recipient capture:
[qqbot] Dispatch event: t=C2C_MESSAGE_CREATE, author.user_openid=<redacted>, content="Proof ping 0614"
known-users: type=c2c, accountId=default, target=qqbot:c2c:<redacted>, openidLength=32, interactionCount=2

Real-recipient QQ text send:
$ node openclaw.mjs message send --channel qqbot --target "qqbot:c2c:<redacted>" --message "OpenClaw PR #92872 real-recipient QQ text proof sha=0bd19b9"
Sent via QQ Bot. Message ID: ROBOT1.0_SXtbE0cfdw9aiRPIzChuFoTJCI6Pyc.VZiElomBe.vCLvO.JYQ2qS9YPXSPekG455knGWIyA0hFu7MXIaP-0tw!!

Real-recipient QQ media send:
pr-92872-qqbot-proof.txt size: 92 bytes
$ node openclaw.mjs message send --channel qqbot --target "qqbot:c2c:<redacted>" --message "OpenClaw PR #92872 real-recipient QQ media proof sha=0bd19b9" --media "pr-92872-qqbot-proof.txt"
Sent via QQ Bot. Message ID: ROBOT1.0_SXtbE0cfdw9aiRPIzChuFq22lWLB2R.1TQiSIxRvl4eN.FADpJpmKMRSTjoYkCL45knGWIyA0hFu7MXIaP-0tw!!

QQBot regression tests on current conflict-resolution update:
Test Files  3 passed (3)
Tests  44 passed (44)

Core/WebChat regression tests on current head `e819629`:
Gateway shard: Test Files 2 passed (2), Tests 16 passed (16)
Infra shard: Test Files 2 passed (2), Tests 65 passed (65)

Lint and bundled-extension lint on current head `c850985`:
corepack pnpm lint --threads=8: passed
corepack pnpm run lint:extensions:bundled: passed

Build on current head `e819629`:
[build-all] phase timings: total 544.0s

check-test-types on current head `c850985`:
$ pnpm tsgo:test
$ pnpm tsgo:core:test && pnpm tsgo:extensions:test
$ node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo
$ node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo
```

- Observed result after fix: OpenClaw resolved and sent the outbound QQBot text and media messages to a real C2C recipient captured from the live QQBot gateway; both sends returned QQBot message IDs. The current conflict-resolution head keeps that implementation rebased on latest `main`, preserves the newly added QQBot markdown-table chunking import alongside the scoped outbound media access import, and passes the targeted QQBot/Core regressions, build, and `check:test-types`.

- What was not tested: group-chat QQBot delivery was not exercised; this proof is scoped to C2C QQBot delivery and WebChat media staging for the issue path. The current update reuses the prior live recipient proof and adds fresh local regression/build/type verification for the conflict-resolution head.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level: Unit test plus local runtime proof
- Target test file: `extensions/qqbot/src/channel.message-adapter.test.ts`, `extensions/qqbot/src/engine/gateway/outbound-dispatch.test.ts`
- Scenario locked in: QQBot adapter and engine preserve scoped outbound media roots for direct media sends, gateway block/tool/streaming dispatch, `<qqmedia>` text tags, structured `QQBOT_PAYLOAD`, scoped `workspaceDir` resolution, and missing-voice staging.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: it verifies the exact boundaries that dropped core media access while keeping QQBot path containment behavior intact.

## Root Cause

- Root cause: the source of truth for sandbox media access is the core outbound `mediaAccess`/`localRoots` contract, but the QQBot channel adapter and media engine did not carry that context into the seven outbound paths — direct text/media, gateway block reply, gateway tool media forwarding, official C2C streaming, `<qqmedia>` text tags, structured `QQBOT_PAYLOAD`, and missing-voice staging. Relative media paths were also resolved against `process.cwd()` instead of `mediaAccess.workspaceDir`, and core `mediaAccess.readFile` was discarded before reaching the low-level senders.
- Missing detection / guardrail: QQBot tests covered regular media dispatch but not sandbox-scoped local media roots, `mediaAccess.readFile` consumption, or scoped `workspaceDir` resolution passed from core outbound context.

## Current review state

Next action: latest `main` conflict resolved and current head `c850985` is ready for maintainer review of the scoped QQBot host-media boundary, including the host-read voice/audio coverage added after local ClawSweeper review.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

First round (clawsweeper review on prior head):

- RF-001 / containment boundary: addressed. The implementation still relies on scoped core-provided roots plus existing local path containment checks; the second-round change routes low-level `sendPhoto`/`sendVideoMsg`/`sendDocument` through the shared outbound loader, which enforces an explicit-`localRoots` guard before any host read. No broad host-file access, config expansion, or QQBot credential shape change was added.
- RF-002 / real recipient proof: addressed. The prior invalid-target proof is superseded by a real C2C recipient capture plus successful text and media sends returning QQBot message IDs (above). Head SHA updated to `69d2a70b`.
- RF-003 / maintainer judgment: acknowledged. The remaining step is maintainer review/merge judgment for the security-boundary-sensitive change, not additional autonomous repair.

Second round (scoped outbound media coverage review on prior head, fixed in `81f2c5dc`):

- RF-004 / gateway block reply media tags (`outbound-dispatch.ts`): fixed. Gateway `dispatchOutbound` now builds a `gatewayMediaContext` from `getAgentScopedMediaLocalRoots(cfg, agentId)` and threads it into `parseAndSendMediaTags`, `sendTextOnlyReply`, and the streaming controller so `<qqmedia>` block replies can resolve scoped workspace media.
- RF-005 / gateway tool media forwarding (`outbound-dispatch.ts`): fixed. The gateway tool `mediaUrl`/`mediaUrls` forwarding path now propagates `mediaLocalRoots`/`mediaReadFile` from the agent scope, so tool-produced workspace files are no longer rejected by containment checks.
- RF-006 / official C2C streaming media target (`streaming-c2c.ts`): fixed. `StreamingMediaContext` now extends `OutboundMediaAccessContext` and `toMediaSendContext` forwards `mediaAccess`/`mediaLocalRoots`/`mediaReadFile` into the media send helper.
- RF-007 / structured `QQBOT_PAYLOAD` local media (`reply-dispatcher.ts`): fixed. `validateStructuredPayloadLocalPath` now resolves the candidate against scoped `localRoots` (via `resolveLocalPathFromRootsSync`) in addition to the QQBot media storage check, so host-mediated workspace media is no longer misclassified as out-of-storage.
- RF-008 / dropped `mediaAccess.readFile` (`outbound-types.ts`): fixed. `OutboundMediaAccessContext` now declares `mediaAccess.readFile` and a top-level `mediaReadFile`; the QQBot adapter no longer discards the host-mediated read capability provided by core.
- RF-009 / relative media path resolution (`outbound-media-send.ts`): fixed. `ResolveOutboundMediaPathOptions` carries `workspaceDir` and `resolveOutboundMediaPath` applies a `resolveWorkspacePathCandidate` step before the existing `resolveMissingPathWithinRoots` containment check, so relative media names are resolved against the scoped workspace rather than `process.cwd()`.
- RF-010 / missing-voice staging scope (`outbound-media-send.ts`): fixed. `sendVoice` now passes `workspaceDir` and the missing-path branch checks both `getQQBotMediaDir()` and the scoped `extraLocalRoots`, so a still-being-written voice file inside a scoped workspace root can reach `waitForFile`. The later RF-013 update also covers host-read voice/audio by staging scoped loader bytes into QQBot-managed media storage before the existing SILK transcode/upload path.

Third round (gateway reply workspace coverage on head `81f2c5dc`, fixed in `69d2a70b`):

- RF-011 / gateway reply missing `mediaAccess.workspaceDir` (`outbound-dispatch.ts`): fixed. `dispatchOutbound` previously built `gatewayMediaContext` with only `mediaLocalRoots`, so a sandboxed block reply that carried a relative media tag (e.g. `<qqmedia>report.docx</qqmedia>`) reached the downstream sender without `mediaAccess.workspaceDir` and was rejected before the file could be resolved against the agent workspace. Gateway now resolves the agent workspace dir via the public plugin-sdk `agent-runtime` helper and passes it through `mediaAccess.workspaceDir` alongside the existing `mediaLocalRoots`, so gateway block reply, tool forwarding, `QQBOT_PAYLOAD`, and official C2C streaming paths share the same scoped workspace resolution as direct outbound sends. A regression test exercises a relative `<qqmedia>` tag in a gateway block reply and confirms it resolves against the configured agent workspace.

Fourth round (local codex review on head `69d2a70b`, fixing the new host-read error path):

- RF-012 / host-read media path missing error-to-OutboundResult conversion (`outbound-media-send.ts`): fixed. The new `trySendViaHostRead` helper ran `loadOutboundMediaFromUrl` and `senderSendMedia` outside any try/catch, so a failure on either call rejected and aborted direct/gateway media delivery instead of returning a structured `OutboundResult.error` like the sibling `sendPhoto`/`sendVideo`/`sendDocument` branches. The helper is now wrapped so host-read or QQ upload failures are converted to `OutboundResult.error`, while `UploadDailyLimitExceededError` still returns the quota-specific `errorCode`/`qqBizCode` metadata via the existing daily-limit result helper. Three regression tests cover `loadOutboundMediaFromUrl` rejection, generic `senderSendMedia` rejection, and daily upload quota metadata preservation.

Fifth round (conflict-resolution update on latest `main`, current head `e819629`):

- Merge conflict: fixed. Latest `main` added QQBot markdown-table chunking in `channel.ts` while this PR threads scoped outbound media access through the same import area; the rebased head keeps both imports and preserves the existing scoped media forwarding behavior.
- RF-001 / RF-002 maintainer boundary decision: still acknowledged as maintainer review scope. The current update does not broaden the already-described scoped host-media boundary; it only rebases the same QQBot scoped-media implementation onto latest `main`.
- RF-003 / old `check-test-types` failure: superseded on current head. `corepack pnpm check:test-types` passes locally on `e819629`.
- RF-004 / RF-005 requested regression commands: rerun and passing on the current conflict-resolution update via the QQBot media tests and core/WebChat media tests listed above.
- RF-006 / build: rerun and passing on the current conflict-resolution update via `corepack pnpm build`.

Sixth round (local ClawSweeper commit review):

- RF-013 / host-read voice/audio gap (`outbound-media-send.ts`): fixed. ClawSweeper found on the prior conflict-resolution head that image/video/file paths consumed scoped `mediaAccess.readFile`, but audio auto-routed to `sendVoice` still relied on plugin direct filesystem access before the SILK transcode/upload path. `sendVoice` now first reads callback-only audio through the shared outbound loader with the existing scoped `mediaAccess`/`mediaLocalRoots`/`mediaReadFile` boundary, stages the bounded bytes under QQBot-managed media storage, and then reuses the existing voice upload path. A regression test covers `clip.mp3` with host-read media access and confirms it reaches the voice sender as staged audio bytes. Local ClawSweeper review on final head `c850985` returned `nothing_found`.

Seventh round (remote check attribution after pushing `e819629`, fixed in `c850985`):

- `check-lint` and `check-additional-extension-bundled`: fixed. Both failed on the same PR-introduced `no-useless-assignment` in `sendVoice`; the final lint update removes the unused initializer without changing voice staging behavior. Local `corepack pnpm lint --threads=8`, `corepack pnpm run lint:extensions:bundled`, QQBot media regressions, and `check:test-types` pass after the update.
- `checks-node-agentic-commands-agent-channel`: attributed as not introduced by this QQBot media change. The remote failure was a timeout in `src/commands/agent.test.ts` for `does not forward agent-scoped to session selectors as delivery targets`, outside the QQBot/media diff. The exact test was rerun locally and passed (`Test Files 1 passed`, `Tests 1 passed | 26 skipped`, duration 54.04s).

Out of scope (acknowledged, not addressed in this PR): group-chat QQBot delivery, additional channels beyond QQBot/WebChat, and any change to the public plugin SDK contract.

target.title: #92077: skills/ClawHub: add sourceUrl to skill metadata schema and CLI display
target.body:
## Summary

Adds a `sourceUrl` field to skill metadata, allowing skill authors to declare a link to their source code repository in `SKILL.md` frontmatter (via `metadata.openclaw.sourceUrl`). This is the first deliverable for ClawHub provenance transparency (ask #1 from the issue).

The field flows through the full pipeline: `OpenClawSkillMetadata` type → frontmatter parser → `SkillStatusEntry` → CLI display (`openclaw skills info`) → gateway protocol schema → ClawHub API client types.

## Root Cause

ClawHub skill listings and `openclaw skills` CLI had no `sourceUrl` or repository field in the skill schema, metadata type, frontmatter parser, or display output. Users could not discover where a skill's source code lives from either the API or the CLI, making informed trust decisions impossible.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Add `sourceUrl` to `OpenClawSkillMetadata` type, parse it from `SKILL.md` frontmatter's `metadata.openclaw.sourceUrl` field, propagate through `SkillStatusEntry`, and surface in `openclaw skills info` CLI output and the gateway protocol schema.

**Real environment tested:** Linux 4.19.112-2.el8.x86_64, node v24.3.0, pnpm 11.2.2, OpenClaw local dev build with real SKILL.md frontmatter parsing pipeline.

**Exact steps or command run after the patch:**
1. Write a `SKILL.md` with `sourceUrl: https://github.com/example/my-skill` in the `metadata.openclaw` frontmatter block
2. Run `./node_modules/.bin/tsx scripts/repro-92077.mjs` to verify frontmatter parsing resolves `sourceUrl` correctly
3. Verify `sourceUrl` appears in `SkillStatusEntry` and propagates to CLI output
4. Run full skills test suite to confirm no regressions

**Evidence after fix:**
```
=== Parsed frontmatter ===
name: my-test-skill
description: A test skill with source URL

=== Resolved metadata ===
homepage: https://example.com
sourceUrl: https://github.com/example/my-skill

✅ sourceUrl correctly parsed from SKILL.md frontmatter

=== Legacy skill (no sourceUrl) ===
sourceUrl: (undefined — optional, backwards compatible)
✅ sourceUrl is optional, no breaking changes
```

Terminal recording (script output) at `/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-issue-92077-evidence/`:
- `repro-output.txt` — plain text output
- `repro-output.png` — terminal screenshot (PNG)
- `test-output.txt` — test results (text)
- `test-output.png` — test results screenshot (PNG)

All 35 skill test suites pass (363 tests), TypeScript compiles with zero type errors, `sourceUrl` is optional — backwards compatible with all existing skills.

**Observed result after fix:**
```
=== Resolved metadata ===
homepage: https://example.com
sourceUrl: https://github.com/example/my-skill

✅ sourceUrl correctly parsed from SKILL.md frontmatter

✅ sourceUrl is optional, no breaking changes
```

Before fix: no `sourceUrl` in schema, metadata, or CLI → After fix: `sourceUrl` is parsed from SKILL.md, propagated through all layers, and surfaced in CLI output. No regressions.

**What was not tested:** N/A — all existing skill tests pass, the change is additive and fully covered.

target.title: feat: add Codex hosted web search
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds a Codex app-server web-search planner that prefers Codex hosted `web_search` when the provider reports support, while keeping OpenClaw's managed `web_search` tool as the fallback for unsupported providers.
- Registers the Codex plugin as an explicit managed web-search provider selectable with `tools.web.search.provider: "codex"`, so any parent model/runtime can delegate a search to a bounded Codex worker without removing an existing provider preset.
- Runs explicit-provider searches in an ephemeral, isolated Codex thread with shell environments, dynamic tools, apps, plugins, image generation, multi-agent, and code mode disabled; it fails closed unless Codex emits a native hosted-search item.
- Keeps native hosted search and managed search mutually exclusive, applies domain/context/location restrictions to Codex thread config, and carries `/btw` side-question policy context into Codex side forks.
- Reuses the bounded Codex lifecycle for media understanding and documents the hosted-search/provider/config boundaries.

## Verification

- `pnpm test extensions/codex/src/app-server/side-question.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/provider-capabilities.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/web-search.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tool-build.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tools.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.binding.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-startup.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/event-projector.test.ts src/agents/btw.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-btw.test.ts src/agents/harness/selection.test.ts -- --reporter=dot` - passed 388 tests across 3 Vitest shards.
- `pnpm test extensions/codex/src/web-search-provider.test.ts extensions/codex/media-understanding-provider.test.ts extensions/codex/index.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/web-search.test.ts` - passed 31 tests across 4 files.
- `pnpm test src/plugins/web-provider-public-artifacts.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/registry.contract.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/loader.contract.test.ts` - passed 26 tests across 3 files and 2 shards.
- `pnpm tsgo:core` - passed.
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions` - passed after the explicit-provider change.
- `pnpm tsgo:test:src` - passed.
- `pnpm build` - passed after the explicit-provider change; emitted the Codex web-search contract artifact and registered manifest provider.
- `git diff --cached --check` before commit - passed.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Codex app-server runs use Codex native hosted `web_search` when supported. OpenClaw users can also explicitly select `tools.web.search.provider: "codex"` while using a different parent model/runtime; that managed tool delegates only the search to an isolated Codex worker.

Real environment tested: Local Rosita OpenClaw agent with isolated config/state and live network access. The explicit-provider proof used parent model a non-Codex parent model, OpenClaw runtime, `tools.web.search.provider: "codex"`, and Codex model `gpt-5.5` for the bounded search worker. A separate native-path proof used the Codex app-server harness with `codex/gpt-5.5`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH="[isolated Rosita config with tools.web.search.provider=codex]" \
OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR="[isolated Rosita state]" \
node dist/index.js agent --local --agent main \
  --session-id rosita-codex-provider-cross-model-20260615T185223 \
  --model [non-Codex-parent-model] \
  --thinking low \
  --timeout 420 \
  --json \
  --message 'Actually invoke the managed web_search tool now; do not merely state intent. Use web_search exactly once and do not use web_fetch or browser. Find two currently operating plumbing service providers serving Edmonton, Alberta. Return each provider name and source URL, then exact marker ROSITA_CODEX_PROVIDER_CROSS_MODEL_OK.'
```

Evidence after fix: The cross-model run reported parent provider/model/harness a non-Codex provider/model using the `openclaw` runtime, exactly one `web_search` call, and zero tool failures. Its persisted tool result reported `provider: "codex"`, `model: "gpt-5.5"`, one hosted search action, and `isError: false`. The earlier native-path Rosita run persisted one Codex `web_search_call` response item and returned its exact marker.

Observed result after fix: The explicit-provider run returned Capital Plumbing & Heating (`https://capitalplumbing.ca/`) and EZ Plumbing (`https://www.ezplumbinginc.ca/`) plus `ROSITA_CODEX_PROVIDER_CROSS_MODEL_OK`. This proves a non-Codex parent model can use Codex as the configured OpenClaw web-search provider without removing another preset to trigger implicit behavior.

What was not tested: Full `pnpm check` and cross-OS Crabbox/Testbox lanes were not run. Final autoreview was intentionally skipped at maintainer request so the local Rosita implementation and live proof could proceed directly.



## Current-main rebase proof

- Rebased the three PR commits onto current upstream `main` (`a447f9a43d`) with zero commits behind at push time; all merge conflicts were resolved against current runtime, plugin SDK, provider, and test contracts.
- Exact-head focused proof: 338 tests passed across five Vitest projects covering Codex hosted search, explicit provider registration, thread lifecycle, side questions, media understanding, harness selection, and plugin contracts.
- Exact-head `pnpm tsgo:core`, `pnpm tsgo:extensions`, branch-wide `pnpm format:check`, and `git diff --check` passed.
- `pnpm build` passed after the current-main reconciliation commit.

## Crabbox live proof

- Date: June 15, 2026
- Successful run: `run_6f65751b4c39`
- Lease/environment: Azure Linux `Standard_D4ads_v6`, lease `cbx_da57866db36d`
- Source synced from branch head: `dcbdfcb74a76e3ad4c969abde38edb4d64a5f0b2`
- Native path: parent `openai/gpt-5.5`, runtime `codex`, native Codex hosted search, one recorded `web_search` call, zero tool failures, and two grounded source URLs from `www.petetheplumber.com` and `mrmikesplumbing.ca`.
- Explicit-provider path: parent `openai/gpt-5.4`, runtime `openclaw`, `tools.web.search.provider: "codex"`, successful `web_search` execution with zero tool failures, and two grounded source URLs from `prontoinc.ca` and `prostarplumbing.ca`.
- Result: both requested live scenarios passed in one isolated Crabbox run. The OpenClaw parent remained on the OpenClaw runtime while the configured Codex provider handled web search.

target.title: fix: deduplicate consecutive assistant messages in session-memory hook
target.body:
## Summary

When using a model with thinking/reasoning enabled (e.g. DeepSeek), the session JSONL may contain two entries for the same assistant response:
1. The raw version with thinking blocks (`[{type: "thinking", text: "..."}, {type: "text", text: "Hi there!"}]`)
2. A cleaned text-only version (`[{type: "text", text: "Hi there!"}]`)

The `session-memory` hook's `getRecentSessionContent()` reads both entries via `extractTextMessageContent()`, which extracts the same text from each. This produces duplicate `assistant:` lines in memory files.

Now the raw thinking entry's cleaned child (linked via `parentId`) is skipped during transcript extraction. Non-consecutive duplicates and unrelated same-text entries (no `parentId` lineage) are preserved. This fix is in the `session-memory` hook only — the storage layer is untouched.

- Problem: session-memory files show every `assistant:` line twice when the model uses thinking/reasoning
- Solution: `getRecentSessionContent()` skips an assistant entry when its `parentId` matches the previous assistant's `id` AND the extracted text is identical
- What changed: `src/hooks/bundled/session-memory/transcript.ts` (+16 lines), new test file `transcript.test.ts` (5 tests)
- What did NOT change: Storage layer, session JSONL format, `session-memory` hook persistence

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix

## Scope

- [x] Memory / hooks

## Linked Issue

- Fixes #92563

## Motivation

Issue #92563 reports duplicate assistant lines in session-memory files when using thinking/reasoning models. The session JSONL stores both a raw message (with thinking blocks + text) and a cleaned text-only version for the same response. `getRecentSessionContent()` reads both via `extractTextMessageContent()`, which extracts text from the first `type: "text"` block — the same text appears in both entries, producing duplicates.

The fix applies deduplication at the transcript extraction layer, constrained to the raw/cleaned assistant **lineage** (via `parentId`): only the cleaned child of a raw thinking entry is skipped. This is the safest location because:
1. It doesn't change storage behavior (affects other consumers like `/new` context)
2. Lineage constraint ensures legitimate repeated assistant text without parent-child relationship is preserved
3. Non-consecutive duplicates are preserved (legitimate repeated messages)

## Changes

**File**: `src/hooks/bundled/session-memory/transcript.ts`

`getRecentSessionContent()` now tracks `lastAssistantId` and `lastAssistantText`. When the next assistant entry's `parentId` matches `lastAssistantId` AND its extracted text matches `lastAssistantText`, it's skipped as the cleaned child of a raw thinking entry. This uses the session entry's lineage (`id`/`parentId`) rather than purely text equality, so unrelated same-text assistant messages are preserved.

**Test**: `src/hooks/bundled/session-memory/transcript.test.ts` (new, 5 tests)

1. Deduplicates consecutive assistant messages with identical text (thinking scenario)
2. Keeps distinct assistant messages unchanged
3. Does not skip non-consecutive duplicates (separated by user message)
4. Does not skip unrelated assistant messages with same text but no `parentId` lineage
5. Does not skip assistant child with different text than parent

## Real Behavior Proof

- Behavior addressed: `getRecentSessionContent()` produces duplicate `assistant:` lines when the session JSONL contains both raw (with thinking blocks) and cleaned (text-only) versions of the same assistant message
- Real environment tested: Linux (RHEL), Node.js 22.22, local checkout on branch `fix/session-memory-dedup-92563` at commit `567858e`

- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
pnpm test src/hooks/bundled/session-memory/transcript.test.ts
pnpm test src/hooks/bundled/session-memory/handler.test.ts
```

- Evidence after fix:

```console
 ✓ |hooks| src/hooks/bundled/session-memory/transcript.test.ts (5 tests) 126ms
 ✓ |hooks| src/hooks/bundled/session-memory/handler.test.ts (23 tests) 784ms

 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  28 passed (28)
```

- Observed result after fix: When the JSONL contains a raw thinking entry followed by its cleaned child (same `parentId` lineage, same text), only the first is included. Unrelated assistant messages with the same text but no `parentId` relationship are preserved. A cleaned child with different text than its parent is preserved. All 5 transcript tests and 23 handler tests pass.
- What was not tested: Live session with a thinking-enabled model and verification of the resulting session-memory file. Requires API key for a reasoning model (e.g. DeepSeek, Claude).

## Risks

- **Low risk**: The change only affects the `session-memory` hook's transcript extraction. It doesn't change storage, JSONL format, or other consumers of session data.
- **Lineage-constrained dedup**: Only skips when the previous assistant entry's `parentId` chain links to the raw thinking entry AND text matches. Unrelated same-text entries are preserved.
- **Regression covered**: 2 new tests for non-lineage duplicates and different-text children.
- **No config/default surface changes**: No new config keys, env vars, or defaults introduced.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Users of thinking/reasoning models (DeepSeek, Claude with thinking enabled, etc.) will no longer see duplicate `assistant:` lines in their session-memory files.

## Security Impact

- [x] New permissions/capabilities? No
- [x] Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- [x] New/changed network calls? No
- [x] Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- [x] Data access scope changed? No

## Human Verification

- Verified scenarios: Consecutive thinking duplicate dedup (test 1), distinct messages preserved (test 2), non-consecutive duplicates preserved (test 3), unrelated same-text entries preserved (test 4), different-text child preserved (test 5)
- Edge cases checked: User message between duplicates (dedup reset), empty text, text starting with `/`, non-assistant messages, `parentId` mismatch, text mismatch with same `parentId`
- What you did NOT verify: Live session with thinking-enabled model

## Compatibility / Migration

- [x] Backward compatible? Yes
- [x] Config/env changes? No
- [x] Migration needed? No

## AI Assistance 🤖

- **AI-assisted**: Yes
- **Human confirmed understanding of code changes**: Yes
- **AI prompts / session excerpts**: Issue #92563 analysis → source code exploration of `getRecentSessionContent()` and `extractTextMessageContent()` in `src/hooks/bundled/session-memory/transcript.ts` → root cause identified as no dedup between consecutive duplicate assistant messages → minimal fix adding consecutive duplicate detection

---

Fixes #92563

target.title: fix(plugins): stop tool-discovery loads from clearing active providers
target.body:
## Summary

- Tool-discovery plugin loads run with `activate: false` and a tool-only plugin scope, so the registry they build omits provider-only plugins.
- `installStandaloneRegistry` unconditionally called `setActivePluginRegistry`, so those loads replaced the live active registry and wiped its `providers`. The gateway `tools-catalog` channel load behind `agents.list` (and the `resolvePluginTools` cold-load) hit this path, so a bundled provider plugin disappeared at runtime after the first tool-discovery load.
- Fix: a tool-discovery load becomes the active registry **only when none exists yet** (cold start). Once an active registry is present, the tool-only registry is only pinned to its requested `channel`/`http-route` surface, so the live providers are preserved. The cold-start promotion still initializes runtime state (cache key / `workspaceDir`), so later surface lookups hit and the bootstrap path does not reload redundantly.
- Also source plugin tool catalog metadata and declared tools from the pinned channel/http-route registry in addition to the active registry, so tool-only plugins keep their display name, description, risk, and tags now that their registry is no longer promoted to active.
- Out of scope: normal activation (`activate !== false`) and the migration provider runtime, which intentionally relies on `setActivePluginRegistry` and does not set `toolDiscovery`.
- Reviewers should focus on `installStandaloneRegistry` (cold-start promotion vs pin-only) and the `tools.catalog` metadata merge.

## Linked context

No linked issue; this is a standalone runtime bug fix. Not requested by a maintainer.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: After the first tool-discovery load (e.g. a gateway `agents.list` / `tools-catalog` call), `getActivePluginRegistry().providers` dropped to an empty array, so a bundled provider-only plugin vanished from the live runtime even though it was loaded at startup.
- Real environment tested: OpenClaw gateway running from a packaged build on Windows, where the defect was first observed through console instrumentation in `setActivePluginRegistry` (active `providers` going from 1 to 0 on the tool-discovery stack). Verified after the patch with a Node reproduction (`tsx`) driving the real loader/runtime source on this branch (Node 22, this checkout).
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `npx tsx __repro_provider_clobber.mts` — it seeds an active registry that carries a provider-only plugin, then drives the real `ensureStandaloneRuntimePluginRegistryLoaded` channel tool-discovery path that the gateway `tools-catalog` / `agents.list` relies on, and prints the active `providers` count before and after.
- Evidence after fix (terminal capture): copied live terminal output after the patch:

```text
REPRO providers BEFORE tool-discovery : 1
REPRO providers AFTER  tool-discovery : 1
REPRO active registry was replaced?   : false
REPRO RESULT                          : PROVIDERS PRESERVED
```

- Observed result after fix: the active registry keeps its provider across a tool-discovery channel load — `providers` stays at 1 and the active registry object is not replaced, so the live provider list survives.
- What was not tested: a full over-the-wire `agents.list` round-trip inside the packaged gateway binary was not re-run after a full rebuild; provider preservation was confirmed through the Node reproduction and the loader regression coverage instead.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: the reproduction drives the real loader/runtime source through `tsx` rather than the fully repackaged gateway binary, because rebuilding and re-syncing the packaged runtime into the host application is out of band for this checkout. The pre-fix symptom itself was captured in the live gateway runtime via console instrumentation.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): same script with the patch stashed (guard removed):

```text
REPRO providers BEFORE tool-discovery : 1
REPRO providers AFTER  tool-discovery : 0
REPRO active registry was replaced?   : true
REPRO RESULT                          : PROVIDERS CLEARED
```

<details>
<summary>Reproduction script used above</summary>

```ts
import { createEmptyPluginRegistry } from "./src/plugins/registry-empty.js";
import {
  getActivePluginRegistry,
  resetPluginRuntimeStateForTest,
  setActivePluginRegistry,
} from "./src/plugins/runtime.js";
import { ensureStandaloneRuntimePluginRegistryLoaded } from "./src/plugins/runtime/standalone-runtime-registry-loader.js";

resetPluginRuntimeStateForTest();

const active = createEmptyPluginRegistry();
active.plugins.push({ id: "qclaw", status: "loaded" } as never);
active.providers.push({ id: "qclaw", pluginId: "qclaw" } as never);
setActivePluginRegistry(active, "startup-key", "default", "/tmp/ws");

console.log("REPRO providers BEFORE tool-discovery :", getActivePluginRegistry()?.providers.length);

ensureStandaloneRuntimePluginRegistryLoaded({
  surface: "channel",
  forceLoad: true,
  loadOptions: {
    config: { plugins: { enabled: true } } as never,
    onlyPluginIds: ["__repro_tool_plugin__"],
    activate: false,
    toolDiscovery: true,
    workspaceDir: "/tmp/ws",
  },
});

const after = getActivePluginRegistry();
console.log("REPRO providers AFTER  tool-discovery :", after?.providers.length);
console.log("REPRO active registry was replaced?   :", after !== active);
console.log(
  "REPRO RESULT                          :",
  after?.providers.length ? "PROVIDERS PRESERVED" : "PROVIDERS CLEARED",
);
```

</details>

## Tests and validation

- Focused suites (all green): `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/runtime/standalone-runtime-registry-loader.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/tools-catalog.test.ts src/plugins/tools.optional.test.ts` → gateway shard 14/14, plugins shard 73/73.
- Types: `pnpm tsgo:core` and `pnpm tsgo:test:src` are clean for the changed files; `git diff --check` is clean.
- Ran the Node reproduction above before and after the patch (see Real behavior proof).
- Regression coverage:
  - loader (`standalone-runtime-registry-loader.test.ts`): a `toolDiscovery` channel load pins the channel surface without replacing an existing active registry; a `toolDiscovery` active load does not replace an existing active registry; a `toolDiscovery` load initializes the active registry when none exists (cold start); a non-`toolDiscovery` active load still installs as the active registry (migration provider path).
  - catalog (`tools-catalog.test.ts`): plugin tool metadata is projected from the pinned channel registry, not only the active registry.
- What failed before this fix: the reproduction showed the active `providers` dropping from 1 to 0 (active registry replaced) before the guard was added.

## Risk checklist

- Did user-visible behavior change? `Yes` — once an active registry exists, provider-only plugins are no longer wiped from it by tool-discovery loads.
- Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? `No` — the migration provider runtime load has no `toolDiscovery` flag and still installs as the active registry exactly as before.
- Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? `No`.
- Highest-risk area: changing when a standalone load becomes globally active (cold-start promotion vs pin-only) and the `tools.catalog` metadata source.
- How is that risk mitigated: full activation (`activate !== false`) and the migration-provider path are unchanged; on a true cold start the promoted tool-only registry is replaced by the first full activation; the loader and `tools.catalog` branches are covered by regression tests.

## Current review state

- Next action: address ClawSweeper's remaining P1 — `tools.catalog` reads the metadata channel registry via `getActivePluginChannelRegistry()`, whose selector can return the active registry instead of a pinned tool-only registry when the active registry has channels and the pinned tool-discovery registry has none. The catalog metadata/declared-tool merge should read the raw pinned channel registry to cover the active-with-channels + pinned-tool-only case (and add a matching regression).
- Copilot review: addressed — the load cache that prevents reloads (`setCachedPluginRegistry` / `getReusableCachedPluginRegistry`) is keyed off the load cache key and is independent of `setActivePluginRegistry`, so skipping the active-registry write does not cause extra plugin reloads.
- ClawSweeper review: the provider-preservation guard and the channel-pinned catalog metadata read are in; the raw-pinned-registry edge above is the open follow-up.

target.title: fix(active-memory): drop assistant chitchat boilerplate from recall summaries
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #84034.

The `active-memory` recall subagent sometimes returned assistant-style chitchat — greetings, "your message got cut off" clarifications, date/time announcements, and help-offer text — when it had nothing useful to recall. `normalizeActiveSummary` accepted any nonempty single line (it only rejected an explicit no-recall token or two timeout-boilerplate patterns), so the chitchat became the "summary" and was injected into `<active_memory_plugin>`, contaminating the main prompt instead of returning no recall.

## What changed

**`extensions/active-memory/index.ts`** — add `CHITCHAT_BOILERPLATE_PATTERNS` (anchored / specific regexes for the greeting / cut-off / date-time / help-offer shapes reported in the issue, in EN + ZH) and an `isChitchatBoilerplateSummary` check; `normalizeActiveSummary` now returns `null` for matching single-line summaries alongside the existing no-recall and timeout-boilerplate checks. `normalizeActiveSummary` is exposed on the `testing` export.

The English greeting opener is **narrowed to two anchored, fail-closed patterns**: (1) a greeting immediately closed by punctuation (`"Hello!"`, `"Hi."`), and (2) a greeting followed by a help/greeting follow-up (`"Hi there"`, `"Hello, how can I help"`, `"Hey how are you"`). A factual summary that merely *begins* with a greeting word — `"Hello Kitty is the user's favorite character"`, `"HelloFresh ..."`, `"Hi-Chew ..."` — is **not** matched and is preserved. This addresses the reviewer's false-positive concern (a broad `^(?:hello|hi|hey)\b\s` opener previously dropped valid name/brand summaries).

**`extensions/active-memory/index.test.ts`** — unit test asserting the six issue-reported chitchat strings normalize to `null`, that genuine factual summaries are preserved, and (new) that greeting-prefixed factual summaries (names/brands such as `Hello Kitty` / `HelloFresh` / `Hi-Chew` / `Hey Arnold`) are kept verbatim rather than dropped.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** `active-memory` recall no longer injects assistant chitchat (greetings, "message got cut off", date/time, help-offers) into `<active_memory_plugin>` — those normalize to no-recall. Genuine factual recall summaries, including ones that begin with a greeting word (names/brands), are still returned unchanged.

**Real environment tested:** Worktree of `origin/main` at PR head `83b9ea7fd5`, Node v22.22.0. Drove the real exported `normalizeActiveSummary` (via the `testing` export) with `node --import tsx` — the actual production normalization, no mocks/stubs.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
```
# Real production normalization on the issue's chitchat + greeting-prefixed factual summaries:
node --import tsx ./harness.mts   # testing.normalizeActiveSummary(...)

# Regression suite:
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/active-memory/index.test.ts
```

**Evidence after fix:** Real console output from `node --import tsx` driving the production `testing.normalizeActiveSummary`:
```
=== CHITCHAT (expect null) ===
OK null  <- 您好！请问有什么可以帮助您的吗？如果您有任何问题或需要帮助，请随时告诉我。
OK null  <- It seems like your message got cut off. Could you please provide
OK null  <- Hello! It looks like your message didn't come through. Could you
OK null  <- 当前日期和时间是 2026-06-02。如果您需要任何帮助，请告诉我。
OK null  <- 您好！看起来您可能没有说出您的问题或请求。
OK null  <- 您好！看起来您的消息可能没有包含具体问题或请求。
=== GREETING-PREFIXED FACTUAL (expect kept verbatim) ===
OK kept  <- Hello Kitty is the user's favorite character.
OK kept  <- HelloFresh is the user's preferred meal-kit service.
OK kept  <- Hi-Chew is the user's favorite candy.
OK kept  <- Hey Arnold is the user's favorite cartoon.
OK kept  <- User prefers TypeScript; deadline 2026-06-15.
OK kept  <- 用户在开发 active-memory 插件，关注 recall 质量。

leaks=0 (want 0), falseDrops=0 (want 0)
```

**Observed result after fix:** All six issue-reported chitchat strings normalize to `null` (no injection); four greeting-prefixed factual summaries (`Hello Kitty` / `HelloFresh` / `Hi-Chew` / `Hey Arnold`) plus two plain factual summaries (EN + ZH) are preserved verbatim. No false positives, no leaks.

**What was not tested:** No live recall subagent / model call — the change is in the deterministic post-processing of the subagent's reply (`normalizeActiveSummary`), exercised directly with the real exported function; the upstream subagent prompt is unchanged.

## Validation (supplemental)

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/active-memory/index.test.ts` → `Test Files 1 passed (1)`, `Tests 149 passed (149)`
- **Negative control:** reverting the narrowed greeting patterns (back to the broad `^(?:hello|hi|hey|greetings)\b[\s!.,]` opener) makes the new false-positive assertions fail — `"Hello Kitty is the user's favorite character."` normalizes to `null` — confirming the regression is real.
- `tsgo:extensions` (`node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.extensions.json`) clean. `git diff --check` clean.
